name: Peking Doilies (Aka Mandarin Pancakes)
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix flour and boiling water until smooth.
2. Gradually add cold water and knead into a smooth dough.
3. Let rest for a few minutes.
4. Roll out the dough into a baguette shape.
5. Cut into six pieces.
6. Flatten each piece with a rolling pin to form 4-inch pancakes.
7. Brush each pancake with sesame oil, placing them together in pairs with oiled sides facing inward.
8. Roll each pair until they are about 6 inches long.
9. Heat a dry nonstick pan over medium-high heat.
10. Cook each pancake pair until golden bubbles form on the underside, shaking the pan occasionally.
11. Flip and cook for another minute or until golden brown on the other side.
12. Remove from the pan and separate the pancakes.
13. Fold into quarters and cover with a warm, damp towel until serving time.
This recipe can be doubled. The pancakes can be made ahead of time and reheated by steaming (though they are best fresh).
